# Transcrição: 01 Ad Formats

- **Fonte:** 01 Ad Formats
- **Duração:** 09:23
- **Idioma:** en

## [00:00 - 03:02]

Alright guys, in this training I'm going to take you through some of the ad formats that are available to you. And so we've got six available ad formats at the moment. In fact, there's actually a little bit more than this and I'll show you why. But the six main ad formats that you have available to you are Canvas ads, Lead ads, Single Image ads, Single Video ads, Carousel ads, and also Collection ads. Now there's separate videos showing you and walking you through exactly how to create each one of these. So let's just quickly have an overview of each of these different ad formats. So as you would suspect, your ad format gets selected at the ad level when you're creating a campaign. So you may be familiar with this scene here where you get to choose the ad format that you are going to use. Now one of the things I just want to point out here guys is with Carousel, Video, and Single Image, you also have an option of actually running these as Canvas ads. So a Canvas is essentially a full screen in Facebook experience. And again, watch the Canvas training video to go into more detail with this. But the front face of a Canvas ad still needs a standard Facebook ad format. So you can either use Carousel, Single Image, or videos for this. So I just wanted to make you aware with that. Now the second thing I just want to point out here is that these ad formats are pretty much available with any campaign objective with the exception of Lead ads. Lead ads require their very own campaign objective. And you will see that at the campaign level when you create a campaign, it will tell you collect leads. So you'll need to select that if you want to run a Lead ad. So let's start off with Carousel. So a Carousel is essentially a stack of images or videos for that matter that people can scroll through. Now you can have up to 10 of these at any one time and you can make them images or you can make them videos. And the format for these are 4x4. And they're very similar to a single image. As you can see, there's a headline and you also have a call to action. But there's a few more options. So these work really, really well for brands or businesses that have lots of different products, lots of different services, lots of different options to choose from. Get creative with this. That's the key with the Carousel ad. You can also work some really clever creative here where you may have seen this already. A single image that is actually split out over four or five cards. So it really entices someone to scroll through. And again, you can watch the Carousel video to find out how to pull that off, how to create

## [03:02 - 06:02]

one of those. The single image ad is probably one of the most commonly used ones because it's just easy. It's really simple to create. And it uses an image that is 1200x628. It's got a headline. It's got a description. It's also can use call to action buttons. And it's got some copy at the top. Very similar to a Carousel with the exception that it's not broken up into cards. Now, I'm not going to talk about the irony here of LinkedIn advertising on Facebook, but you can see the example on the right hand side of a single image ad. One of the things I quickly want to point out here is that the call to action button. So you'll see the learn more on that example. If you're running a traffic objective campaign or you're optimizing your ad delivery for link clicks, you will require a call to action button. You cannot have your ad and not have a call to action button. So that's just a little caveat I just wanted to point out here. Otherwise, you can choose to omit that call to action button or choose to have it. Slide show ads are essentially scrolling images and you need to pick between three to ten images to be used in here. You can also add music, which is a really cool feature to slide show ads. Now these can be run almost the same way as video in the sense that you can build a custom audience of people that viewed your slide show to retarget them. So it's extremely powerful in that manner. And also it's a lot easier to create than it is to create a video. So if you don't quite have the resources or budget to create a video, highly recommend you run a slide show because Facebook really is favoring this ad format. It stands out in the news feed because it's moving and you can also build up custom audiences to retarget. The collection ad format is a brand new ad format that Facebook recently rolled out. And you may or may not have seen this before. It essentially can use a video or a single image at the top as you'll see here with the woman's feet. That could be a video or a single image. And the difference with a collection is that down at the bottom what you'll see in the example is it's almost like a carousel of all the different products. Now one of the things that's important about creating a collection ad format is that you actually need to create a product set. So first you need to have a product set in order for Facebook to pull these different images down below. So that's just a little bit of a work around that you need to do if you want to use this type of ad format. And again you can have a slideshow, a video or an image at the top image here. And then we've got video.

## [06:02 - 09:03]

So video is also one of the most popular used ad formats out there. And the reason for that is quite evident. Facebook loves video. It's really, really preferring video over any other ad format at the moment. It allows for really easy retargeting. And if you've got the creative it's definitely an effective way to build top of funnel awareness and consideration. Now with your video you'll just need to upload that and you can have anything as long as 60 minutes. So it's quite long. But of course with social media with Facebook you want to try to keep things a little bit less than that. Another thing I want to mention is when you upload a video you now have an option to automatically add captions to the video. So Facebook will automate captions. You probably will need to go through and edit it because it's not the most trustworthy captions on the planet. But it saves you a lot of time and headache from having to go on Fiverr and getting other people to do it. And of course 80% of people that watch videos on Facebook don't do so with sound on. So they listen to it with the sound off. So if you've got talking in your video make sure you're using captions for ultimate engagement. And lastly we've got Canvas and lead ads. We've got the Canvas here on the right hand side and the lead ad example on the left hand side. A lead ad is the one I was telling you needs a different campaign objective. So you need to run with the lead objective. Now this one actually collects emails and information straight from Facebook. You don't have to send them to a landing page or to a website. They can fill out a form and in fact that form is often pre-populated and just submit it and you can collect all the information and add that to your database or send them freebies or offers or anything like this. It's a very, very, very, very effective and easy way to test or validate a funnel before you go and build out lots of landing pages and all the funnel steps behind that. And then what you'll see here with the Canvas. A Canvas ad can, as I mentioned, use slideshows, videos, single image. But the difference here is when someone taps on the Canvas ad and this can only be used on mobile, a Canvas. When someone taps on the Canvas essentially it will open up within Facebook. So it's almost like having a mini website inside of Facebook. It never takes them off Facebook. Facebook loves Canvas because it protects the user experience, right? There's no loading times. There's no bounce rates. It's a highly controlled and optimized environment that Facebook has created. And because of this, Canvas ads behave a little bit differently in terms of the CPMs that you're going to see or the results that you'll get from them.

## [09:03 - 09:23]

So definitely a little bit more work to create a Canvas, but it's a lot easier than creating a website itself. So it's definitely worth exploring Canvas ads. Now what I want to show you in the next video is how to find each of these different ad IDs and how to create campaigns that are using existing ads.
